title: Visualize Qsim Output
text: details install KWB-R packages checkout installation tutorial.
code: ### Optionally: specify GitHub Personal Access Token (GITHUB_PAT) ### See here why this might be important for you: ### https://kwb-r.github.io/kwb.pkgbuild/articles/install.html#set-your-github_pat  # Sys.setenv(GITHUB_PAT = "mysecret_access_token")  # Install package "remotes" from CRAN if (! require("remotes")) {   install.packages("remotes", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org") }  # Install KWB package 'qsimVis' from GitHub remotes::install_github("KWB-R/qsimVis")

what: Adds the river ID of qsimVis package to the table — add_qsimVis_id

text: River ID can either defined verknet data (see details) manually entered. output tables lists source river information defined size river plotting additional columns.

title: Adds the river ID of qsimVis package to the table — add_qsimVis_id
Adds the river ID of qsimVis package to the table — add_qsimVis_id
text: aggregated_data input dataframe. Either manually entered created one deviating_hours(),adverse_deviation_from_reference() critical_events() translation_table Project specific translation dataframe contains verknet data project specific IDs. Column names must "ID": Verknet ID ID manually added rivers also filnames river csv tables "verknet_BWaStrIdNr": water body number verknet. NA river information assumed added manually. "Bezeichnung": real water body name "section_name": project specific ID whole river river section. Can multiple IDs per waterbody, separated ",", however, must unique.

title: Relative adverse deviation from a reference — adverse_deviation_from_reference
Relative adverse deviation from a reference — adverse_deviation_from_reference
text: dataFrame Data frame time first column parameter values per site. reference data frame provided must include reference_vector column. reference Column name reference dataFrame seperate data frame contains column names dataFrame worst numerical value worst case good_values Character, either "high" "low" defining best

title: Relative adverse deviation from a reference — adverse_deviation_from_reference
text: Numeric value 0 1

title: Relative adverse deviation from a reference — adverse_deviation_from_reference
text: First similarity data vector reference vector calculated. complete pairs (NA values) used. data pair quotient data reference calcutaled. data > reference value set 1. quotients  cumulated (-> absolute similarity). can maximum number data pairs. deviding number data pairs, relative similarity obtained. One minus relative similarity negative deviation.

title: Count Events of deficits — critical_events
text: Counts Number intervals minimum time row defined threshold value. Events separated specified period time threshold. Furthermore, exceedance value can also separation criterion.

title: Count Events of deficits — critical_events
code: critical_events(   dataFrame,   separating_hours,   deficiency_hours,   threshold,   recovery_value = NULL,   return_event_positions = FALSE )

title: Count Events of deficits — critical_events
text: dataFrame Data frame time first column parameter values per site. separating_hours Minimal time period two values threshold count separate events. deficiency_hours Minimal time period values threshold. threshold Numeric unit data vector recovery_value NULL (default) two events separated numerical revocvery value exceeded two deficits. return_event_positions Instead number events, events starting end positions returned, corresponding data vector

title: Count Events of deficits — critical_events
text: Either number events data frame event start end position

title: Count Events of deficits — critical_events
text: deficiency_hours seperating_hours lead decimal number data points, numerical rounded next integer. example: minimum deficiency time 20 minutes 15 minute resolution mean least 2.3 data points must threshold values rounded 2 data points, deficiency time 25 minutes result 3 data points needed threshold.

what: Adds quality categories to the river table — extend_riverTable
title: Adds quality categories to the river table — extend_riverTable
text: result, location linked water quality value.

title: Adds quality categories to the river table — extend_riverTable
code: extend_riverTable(   rivers,   river_id,   aggregated_data,   varName,   sixBreaks,   NA_processing = "interpolation" )

title: Adds quality categories to the river table — extend_riverTable
text: rivers List rivers loaded load_rivers() river_id Character verknet river ID one names rivers list aggregated_data dataframe created one deviating_hours(), adverse_deviation_from_reference() critical_events() varName column name agregated data output_table sixBreaks Breaks defining lower limits categories. NA_processing Either "interpolation" "steps". Defines NA values two locations either interpolated (default) kept constant based upstream value.

title: Adds quality categories to the river table — extend_riverTable
text: input river_table extended three columns: 1) "value" containing exact value, 2) "quality" containing 6 different quality categories based defined breaks, 3) "color" assigning th color plotting

title: Adds quality categories to the river table — extend_riverTable
text: qsim_misa_table provide information every location within river, since local resolution qsim output differs river course data. Values two known river sites interpolated. color scale changed. Since misa assessment parameters low values refer good water quality, colors range green low values red high values.

title: Calculates x- and y-scale based on longitude and lattitude data — getDimensions
text: ratio width height directly based ratio lonigtude lattitude. order get correct scale plot margins need set 0 (see example).

title: Calculates x- and y-scale based on longitude and lattitude data — getDimensions
code: xlim <- c(13.18, 13.472) ylim <- c(52.46, 52.57) plotDim <- getDimensions(xlim = xlim, ylim = ylim, width = 10) plotDim #>     width    height  #> 10.000000  6.182557   dev.new(noRStudioGD = TRUE, width = plotDim[1], height = plotDim[2]) par(mar = c(0, 0, 0, 0)) # no margins outside of the plot plot(x = xlim, y = ylim, type = "l") text(x = mean(xlim), y = ylim[2],      labels = "Correctly Scaled Line",      pos = 1, cex = 3)

what: Load a package geo file — load_geo
title: Load a package geo file — load_geo
text: package geo files stored Rdata three objects gis_coordinates (geomitries), gis_data (everything except geometries) readme (list information)

title: Load a package geo file — load_geo
Load a package geo file — load_geo
text: region region Geo data defined package extdata folder Rdata_file name file (without ".RData")

title: Reads river data — load_rivers
text: Reads csv files starting "river" folder combines list. csv defines river stretch region interest must least contain 5 columns: x: longitude y: lattitude distance_to_neighbour: distance previous point river km km: distance river estuary qsim_id: qsim ID given function QSIM_prepare() point matches qsim output site

title: Reads river data — load_rivers
Reads river data — load_rivers
text: aggregated_data dataframe created one deviating_hours(), adverse_deviation_from_reference() critical_events() verknet IDs rownames translation_table Project specific translation dataframe contains verknet data project specific IDs. Column names must "ID": Verknet ID ID manually added rivers also filnames river csv tables "verknet_BWaStrIdNr": water body number verknet. NA river information assumed added manually. "Bezeichnung": real water body name "section_name": project specific ID whole river river section. Can multiple IDs per waterbody, separated ",", however, must unique. "river_size": Information line width drawn 1 (thin) 3 (big). path_manual path manually added river tables. NULL (default) manually added rivers assumed.

title: Creates an map Plot, dimensions based on rivers and Qsim output data — plot_empty_map
text: bbox List Bounding box two vectors length 2 c(x_min, x_max) c(y_min, y_max). NULL, rivers used define bounding box. bbox overwrites rivers bounding box definition. rivers List data frames containing river stretch (longitude latitude) values plotted. Created functions load_rivers() extend_riverTable() plot_toner TRUE toner map used background

title: Splits the Qsim ID into 4 differnt informations — site_info_from_qsimID
